title: WOS API Provenance
description: Public API resource schema for WOS provenance records. ProvenanceRecord is a tier-discriminated union over Facts (kernel S8), Reasoning (governance S6.2), Counterfactual (governance S6.4), and Narrative (AI integration S13). The wire shape is a closed oneOf keyed by `tier` -- each variant is a complete object with no shared envelope, so codegen produces a typed Rust enum and a typed TypeScript discriminated union without hand-edits (ADR 0082 D-5, Notes section). Identity is carried by the URN `actorRef` (D-9); the legacy nested `actor: {id,type,name}` shape is not expressible.

FactsRecordKind.description: Facts-tier record kind. The full closed reserved-literal set is aligned with `schemas/record-kind-registry.json` (132 ProvenanceKind variants, all camelCase). 22 carry schema-validated payload shapes (if/then or $def overlay); the remaining 110 are flat Facts-tier records. The `^x-[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$` extension arm adds vendor extensibility (ADR 0082 D-12). Literal-set parity against the Rust `ProvenanceKind` enum is enforced by `scripts/check-recordkind-parity.py`.

SourceAuthority.description: Authority rank for an applied rule (governance S6.2). Closed taxonomy (ADR 0082 D-12). `type: string` matches the kernel `wos-workflow.schema.json#/$defs/RuleReference.properties.sourceAuthority` projection.

EvidenceKind.description: Kind of evidence cited in a Reasoning-tier record (governance S6.2). Closed taxonomy. `type: string` matches the kernel `wos-workflow.schema.json#/$defs/EvidenceReference.properties.kind` projection.

CounterfactualPolarity.description: Counterfactual polarity (governance S6.4). `positive` records controllable factors that would change the outcome; `negative` records irrelevant factors that did not affect the outcome.

RuleReference.description: Reference to an authoritative rule applied in a decision. Mirrors `wos-workflow.schema.json#/$defs/RuleReference` (governance S6.2). Inlined at the API layer so codegen does not need a cross-schema resolver; structurally aligned so the API projection is byte-compatible with the kernel definition.

ruleId.description: Rule identifier (statute section, regulation paragraph, policy ID).

examples: Annual renewal of eligibility required for continued benefit receipt.

citation.description: Formal citation to the source document.

EvidenceReference.description: Reference to a piece of evidence consulted in a Reasoning-tier record. Mirrors `wos-workflow.schema.json#/$defs/EvidenceReference` (governance S6.2). At least one of `caseFieldPath` or `uri` MUST be present so the evidence is locatable; the `anyOf` constraint is a load-bearing structural pin enforced by Gate 6 byte-parity (PLN-0403 path b).

caseFieldPath.description: JSON pointer / dotted path into the case file (when `kind` is `caseField`).

uri.description: URI of the external document or record (when `kind` is `document` or `externalRecord`).

summary.description: Short human-readable description of what this evidence shows.
examples: Updated medical assessment from treating physician dated 2026-03-14.

CriterionResult.description: Single criterion check recorded in a Reasoning-tier record (governance S6.2).

criterion.description: Identifier or short label for the criterion that was checked.

satisfied.description: True when the criterion was satisfied by the case facts.

detail.description: Optional human-readable detail for the criterion result.

DecisionRequirements.description: Required-input and applicable-rule declaration for a decision (governance S6.3).

requiredInputs.description: Data fields required for this decision.

applicableRules.description: Rules governing this decision, with regulatory citations.

effectiveDate.description: Date as of which rules and thresholds apply.

Counterfactual.description: Single counterfactual entry recorded in a Counterfactual-tier record (governance S6.4).

polarity.description: Whether this counterfactual is positive (would change outcome) or negative (did not affect outcome).

factor.description: Short identifier or label for the factor.

effect.description: Human-readable description of the counterfactual effect or non-effect.
examples: If reported income were $1,500 lower, applicant would qualify.
Applicant race did not factor into the eligibility decision.

AssertionGateKind.description: Closed-with-vendor-extension assertion-gate kind (governance §5.4 Assertion Gates; workflow-governance.md:293-305). Reserved literals enumerate the seven gate kinds the spec table at §5.4 normates: `source-grounded` (extracted values must appear in source), `arithmetic` (recomputation check), `range` (bounds check), `consistency` (cross-stage non-contradiction), `format` (parse check), `cross-document` (multi-document consistency), `temporal` (date/time ordering). Vendor extensions MUST use an `^x-[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$` prefix per ADR 0082 D-12. A regulator querying "every pipeline stage that failed an `arithmetic` gate" reads this taxonomy directly.

AssertionGateResult.description: Single assertion-gate evaluation result (governance §5.4; workflow-governance.md:293-305). Each pipeline stage's gate evaluation produces one of these — closed-taxonomy gate kind + pass/fail boolean + structured evidence references. Embedded as an optional array on `ReasoningTierRecord.assertionGates` so an auditor walking the reasoning trace can answer "which gate kind failed at this stage and what evidence backs the failure?" without re-deriving from open-prose `criteriaChecked.detail`.

gateKind.description: Closed-with-vendor-extension classification of which §5.4 gate produced this result.

gatePassed.description: True when the gate's verification method (governance §5.4 third column) returned pass; false when it returned fail. The pipeline's risk profile is determined by the weakest gate (workflow-governance.md:309), so any false here load-bears.

evidenceRefs.description: Structured evidence references supporting the gate's pass/fail finding (governance §5.4 — "each gate produces a pass/fail result with structured evidence"). Empty when the gate verification did not surface specific evidence references (typical for `format` parse checks); populated for `source-grounded`, `cross-document`, and `consistency` gates whose evidence is referenceable.

stageId.description: Optional pipeline-stage identifier this gate evaluation belongs to. Useful when multiple gates fire in one stage and consumers want to correlate by stage.

detail.description: Optional human-readable detail explaining the gate result. Free-form prose for cases where the structured evidence does not carry the full story.

ConfidenceReport.description: Per-output confidence record (ai-integration.md §7.1; ai-integration.md:419-431 — "Every agent output MUST be accompanied by a ConfidenceReport"). Closed structural shape carrying the agent's calibrated probability that the output is correct, decay state, and the cumulative-confidence rolling window for multi-step interactions (ai-integration.md §7.7). Embedded as an optional field on Reasoning-tier and Narrative-tier records when an agent produced the output the record describes.

outputId.description: Stable identifier of the agent output this report covers. Lets a single record carry multiple `ConfidenceReport` instances when an agent emits multiple outputs in one invocation.

confidence.description: Calibrated probability in [0,1] that this output would be accepted without modification by a competent human reviewer (ai-integration.md:430). Floor enforcement compares against `AgentView.confidenceFloor`.

decayApplied.description: True when confidence decay has reduced the effective confidence below the agent's reported confidence (ai-integration.md §7.5). Distinguishes "agent reported 0.9 and that's the live value" from "agent reported 0.95 and decay halved it to 0.475".

method.description: Closed-with-vendor-extension confidence-derivation method (ai-integration.md §7.2; ai-integration.md:434-440). Reserved literals: `modelNative` (model probability estimate), `calibrated` (post-hoc calibration on historical accuracy), `heuristic` (output-structure properties), `conformal` (conformal prediction sets), `declared` (developer-assigned). Vendor extensions MUST use an `^x-[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$` prefix per ADR 0082 D-12.

cumulativeConfidenceWindow.description: Rolling cumulative-confidence state (ai-integration.md §7.7; ai-integration.md:475-477 — "the cumulative confidence after step n is the product of individual step confidences by default"). Lets a reviewer query "is this multi-step session below the confidence floor?" without re-deriving the product.

stepCount.description: Number of steps included in the cumulative product.

cumulativeConfidence.description: Product of per-step confidences in [0,1]. When this falls below the workflow's `confidenceFloor`, the §7.7 MUST requires the interaction to pause for human review.

floor.description: Confidence floor in [0,1] this window is checked against (ai-integration.md §7.4). Surfaced inline so consumers do not need to resolve `AgentView.confidenceFloor` separately.

below.description: True when `cumulativeConfidence < floor` — the load-bearing observable that triggers the §7.7 pause-for-human-review MUST.

fieldLevel.description: Optional per-field confidence breakdown (ai-integration.md §7.3 Per-Field Confidence; ai-integration.md:443-444). Lets routing logic reference individual output fields rather than the rolled-up `confidence` value.

fieldName.description: Output field name this entry covers.

AssembledExplanation.description: Server-assembled adverse-decision explanation (governance §3.8; workflow-governance.md:166-238). The §3.8.1 algorithm's output structure — Reasoning records ordered by authority rank + Counterfactual records partitioned positive/negative + assembly metadata — projected as a typed read-only resource so clients do not re-implement the deterministic-assembly MUST. Returned by `GET /api/v1/cases/{case_id}/processes/{process_id}/explanation`. Two conformant processors MUST produce the byte-identical assembled explanation from the same provenance log (workflow-governance.md:170).

instanceId.description: Case instance URN this explanation was assembled for. Entity-type segment MUST be `instance`.

assembledAt.description: RFC 3339 timestamp the assembly algorithm ran (governance §3.8.1 step 5 `assembledAt`).

assembledBy.description: Actor URN that ran the assembly. Typically a `workload` principal-class actor representing the runtime; manual re-assembly by a human is permitted and would carry that actor's URN.

narrative.description: Structured natural-language explanation rendered from the ordered reasoning + counterfactual material (governance §3.8.3 — "a JSON structure, not rendered text" applies to the underlying records; this `narrative` is the rolled-up prose the host renders for the applicant). Open prose.

factsTrace.description: Ordered URNs of the Facts-tier provenance records the assembly walked (governance §3.8.1 step 1 — `provenanceLog.filter(tier="facts")` ordering preserved). Lets clients verify the explanation's input set against the per-instance provenance feed.

rulesApplied.description: Rules applied across the determination, ordered by authority rank descending (governance §3.8.2 — `statute > regulation > policy > guideline`). Cross-`$ref`s `RuleReference` from this same schema rather than redefining (ADR 0082 D-14).

positiveCounterfactuals.description: Controllable factors that would change the outcome (governance §3.8.1 step 3, §6.4). Empty for non-rights-impacting workflows.

negativeCounterfactuals.description: Irrelevant factors — including protected characteristics — that did NOT affect the outcome (governance §3.8.1 step 3, §6.4).

transitionId.description: Optional identifier of the transition that produced the adverse decision (governance §3.8.3). Surfaced for cross-reference against the instance's lifecycle log; omitted when assembly was triggered outside a single-transition context.

CaseFileSnapshot.description: Canonical case-file snapshot captured at determination time (kernel S8.2.1). Mirrors `wos-workflow.schema.json#/$defs/CaseFileSnapshot`.

value.description: Raw case-file JSON value observed immediately before the determination transition fires.

sha256.description: Lowercase SHA-256 hex digest of jcsCanonical.

IntegrityDigest.description: Tamper-detection digest pair for a provenance record (kernel S8.3). Optional; servers SHOULD include it when the runtime stamps digests.

algorithm.description: Digest algorithm. Closed taxonomy with vendor-prefix extension.

FactsTierRecord.description: Facts-tier provenance record (kernel S8). Authoritative, immutable layer recording what happened. The wire shape carries the kernel `FactsTierRecord` envelope; the API layer renames `id`, `instanceId`, `actorRef` to URNs (D-4, D-9) and exposes `recordKind` as a closed enum (with vendor extension). Each tier variant is fully self-contained -- no shared envelope or `allOf` composition -- so codegen produces a clean discriminated union.

id.description: Stable URN for this provenance record.

instanceId.description: Case instance URN this record belongs to.

recordKind.description: Kernel-defined kind of facts record.

timestamp.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the recorded action occurred (ADR 0069).

definitionVersion.description: Version of the governing Kernel Document at append time.

actorRef.description: Actor URN responsible for the action (D-9). Omitted when the processor cannot name a concrete actor.

event.description: Triggering event or reserved event literal carried by the record kind.

fromState.description: Lifecycle state immediately before the recorded transition.

toState.description: Lifecycle state immediately after the recorded transition.

lifecycleState.description: Lifecycle state at the time of the action.

transitionTags.description: Semantic tags copied from the firing transition (kernel S4.9.12). REQUIRED when the firing transition carries tags.

caseFileSnapshot.description: Canonical case-file snapshot. REQUIRED when transitionTags include `determination` (kernel S8.2.1).

outcome.description: Reserved outcome literal recorded by the processor (kernel S8.2.2). Open enum with vendor-prefix extension.

inputs.description: Input entity references used by this activity.

outputs.description: Output entity references generated by this activity.

data.description: Record-kind-specific structured payload. Discriminated by `recordKind`. Common fields surface here; kind-specific structured contracts are pinned via the dedicated typed sub-shape fields (`overrideRecord`, `noticeSent`) and the kernel `CapabilityInvocationRecord` `invocationBlocked` if/then guard. Carries the AI §3.3.1 `invocationBlocked` discriminator for `capabilityInvocation` records, AI §3.4 `agentVersionChange` `priorVersion` / `newVersion`, AI §5.5 `autonomyDemotion` `trigger` / `newLevel`, AI §7.5 `confidenceDecay` `halfLife` / `effectiveConfidence`, AI §7.7 cumulative-confidence pause `cumulative` / `floor`, governance §7.1 `samplingDecision` `method` / `bucket`, advanced equity guardrail breach (`equityAlert`) carrying `guardrailId`, `protectedCategoryId`, `disparityScore`, `threshold`, and `occurredAt`, and similar lightweight payloads. Heavier structured contracts (override, notice) MUST use the dedicated typed sub-shape fields above.

invocationBlocked.description: AI §3.3.1: true when the processor declined to invoke the capability because a precondition evaluated false. Pairs with `outcome: "preconditionNotSatisfied"` on the parent envelope. Present only when `recordKind == "capabilityInvocation"`.

capabilityId.description: AI §3.3.1: identifier of the capability being invoked or blocked. Present only when `recordKind == "capabilityInvocation"`.

milestoneId.description: Kernel S4.13: identifier of the milestone that fired. Present only when `recordKind == "milestoneFired"`.

timerId.description: Kernel S9.7.7: identifier of the timer. Present only when `recordKind` is `timerCreated`, `timerCancelled`, or `timerFired`.

priorVersion.description: AI §3.4: prior model version string before the change. Present only when `recordKind == "agentVersionChange"`.

newVersion.description: AI §3.4: new model version string after the change. Present only when `recordKind == "agentVersionChange"`.

trigger.description: AI §5.5 / §7.5: identifier of the trigger that fired (autonomy demotion trigger, confidence decay trigger). Open-string with named seam for codegen.

newLevel.description: AI §5.5: effective autonomy level after demotion. Closed enum mirrors `governance.schema.json#/$defs/AutonomyLevel`.

halfLife.description: AI §7.5: ISO 8601 duration string for the confidence half-life. Present only when `recordKind == "confidenceDecay"`.

effectiveConfidence.description: AI §7.5 / §7.7: effective confidence after decay or cumulative computation. Present on `confidenceDecay` and `cumulativeConfidenceViolation` records.

cumulative.description: AI §7.7: cumulative-confidence value at the moment of pause. Present only when `recordKind == "cumulativeConfidenceViolation"`.

floor.description: AI §7.4: confidence floor against which `effectiveConfidence` / `cumulative` was compared.

method.description: Governance §7.1 / AI §11.2: sampling method used to select this case for review. Present only when `recordKind == "samplingDecision"`. Closed-with-vendor-extension.

bucket.description: Governance §7.1 / AI §11.2: stratification bucket identifier when `method == "stratified"`.

constraintId.description: AI §4.7: identifier of the deontic constraint being bypassed. Present only when `recordKind == "deonticBypass"`.

rationale.description: AI §4.7: structured rationale for a deontic bypass. Present only when `recordKind == "deonticBypass"`. Distinct from the heavier `OverrideRecord.rationale` which carries citations.

fallbackOption.description: AI §8.2.4: fallback option taken on this attempt. Present only when `recordKind` is `fallbackTriggered`, `fallbackAttempt`, or `fallbackTerminal`.

attemptIndex.description: AI §8.2.4: zero-based index of this fallback attempt within the chain. Present only when `recordKind == "fallbackAttempt"`.

succeeded.description: AI §8.2.4: whether this fallback attempt succeeded. Present only when `recordKind == "fallbackAttempt"`.

zoneId.description: Advanced §4: identifier of the constraint zone whose satisfaction is being recorded. Present only when `recordKind == "zoneSatisfied"`.

agentRef.description: AI / advanced: agent URN this record applies to. Present on AI lifecycle / drift / autonomy / fallback records.

^x-[a-z]+-.$comment: Vendor-extension fields permitted under the closed-with-vendor-extension stance (ADR 0082 D-12).

overrideRecord.description: Typed payload when `recordKind == "overrideRecorded"` (governance §7.3). Carries the structured rationale, authority verification, and supporting evidence required for an override to be auditable. Optional only because the wire envelope is shared across all Facts-tier kinds; servers emitting `overrideRecorded` MUST populate this.

noticeSent.description: Typed payload when `recordKind == "noticeSent"` (governance §3.2, workflow-governance.md:127). Carries the byte-deterministic notice metadata so applicants can retrieve the notice that was actually sent with verifiable hash equality. Optional only because the wire envelope is shared across all Facts-tier kinds; servers emitting `noticeSent` MUST populate this.

authorizationRejectedRecord.description: Typed payload when `recordKind == "authorizationRejected"` (ADR 0070 D-3). Optional only because the wire envelope is shared across all Facts-tier kinds; servers emitting `authorizationRejected` MUST populate this.

legalHoldRecord.description: Typed payload when `recordKind` is `legalHoldPlaced`, `legalHoldReleased`, or `legalHoldDestructionRejected` (governance §12 legal holds). Optional only because the wire envelope is shared across all Facts-tier kinds; servers emitting any legal-hold literal MUST populate this.

circuitBreakerEvent.description: Typed payload when `recordKind` is `circuitBreakerTripped` or `circuitBreakerReset` (advanced-governance.md circuit breaker). Optional only because the wire envelope is shared across all Facts-tier kinds; servers emitting a circuit-breaker literal MUST populate this.

shadowModeDivergence.description: Typed payload when `recordKind == "shadowModeDivergence"` (advanced-governance.md shadow mode). Optional only because the wire envelope is shared across all Facts-tier kinds; servers emitting `shadowModeDivergence` MUST populate this.

dcrZoneViolation.description: Typed payload when `recordKind == "dcrZoneViolation"` (advanced-governance.md DCR constraint zones). Optional only because the wire envelope is shared across all Facts-tier kinds; servers emitting `dcrZoneViolation` MUST populate this.

driftAlert.description: Typed payload when `recordKind == "driftAlert"` (advanced-governance.md drift monitoring). Optional only because the wire envelope is shared across all Facts-tier kinds; servers emitting `driftAlert` MUST populate this.

reportTimedOut.description: Typed payload when `recordKind == "reportTimedOut"` (reports.md WOS-1408). Optional only because the wire envelope is shared across all Facts-tier kinds; servers emitting `reportTimedOut` MUST populate this.

quorumReduction.description: Typed payload when a governance operation explicitly reduces a quorum requirement. Optional only because the wire envelope is shared across all Facts-tier kinds; servers recording quorum reduction MUST populate this and use an explicit policy-transition `recordKind` rather than silently changing quorum state.

oneOf.description: D26 agreement guard: caseCreated selects the case-created event literal.

recordKind.description: D26 agreement guard source: case-created facts record kind.

event.description: D26 agreement guard target: case-created event literal.

oneOf.description: D26 agreement guard: intakeAccepted selects the intake-accepted event literal.

recordKind.description: D26 agreement guard source: intake-accepted facts record kind.

event.description: D26 agreement guard target: intake-accepted event literal.

oneOf.description: D26 agreement guard: intakeRejected selects the intake-rejected event literal.

recordKind.description: D26 agreement guard source: intake-rejected facts record kind.

event.description: D26 agreement guard target: intake-rejected event literal.

oneOf.description: D26 agreement guard: intakeDeferred selects the intake-deferred event literal.

recordKind.description: D26 agreement guard source: intake-deferred facts record kind.

event.description: D26 agreement guard target: intake-deferred event literal.

oneOf.description: D26 agreement guard: forEachIterationStarted selects the ForEach iteration-start event literal.

recordKind.description: D26 agreement guard source: ForEach iteration-start facts record kind.

event.description: D26 agreement guard target: ForEach iteration-start event literal.

oneOf.description: D26 agreement guard: forEachIterationCompleted selects the ForEach iteration-completed event literal.

recordKind.description: D26 agreement guard source: ForEach iteration-completed facts record kind.

event.description: D26 agreement guard target: ForEach iteration-completed event literal.

oneOf.description: D26 agreement guard: forEachCompleted selects the ForEach completed event literal.

recordKind.description: D26 agreement guard source: ForEach completed facts record kind.

event.description: D26 agreement guard target: ForEach completed event literal.

oneOf.description: D26 agreement guard: signatureAffirmation selects the signature-affirmation event literal.

recordKind.description: D26 agreement guard source: signature-affirmation facts record kind.

event.description: D26 agreement guard target: signature-affirmation event literal.

oneOf.description: D26 agreement guard: signatureAdmissionFailed selects the signature-admission-failed event literal.

recordKind.description: D26 agreement guard source: signature-admission-failed facts record kind.

event.description: D26 agreement guard target: signature-admission-failed event literal.

oneOf.description: D26 agreement guard: determinationRescinded selects the determination-rescinded event literal.

recordKind.description: D26 agreement guard source: determination-rescinded facts record kind.

event.description: D26 agreement guard target: determination-rescinded event literal.

oneOf.description: D26 agreement guard: reinstated selects the reinstated event literal.

recordKind.description: D26 agreement guard source: reinstated facts record kind.

event.description: D26 agreement guard target: reinstated event literal.

oneOf.description: D26 agreement guard: clockStarted selects the clock-started event literal.

recordKind.description: D26 agreement guard source: clock-started facts record kind.

event.description: D26 agreement guard target: clock-started event literal.

oneOf.description: D26 agreement guard: clockResolved selects the clock-resolved event literal.

recordKind.description: D26 agreement guard source: clock-resolved facts record kind.

event.description: D26 agreement guard target: clock-resolved event literal.

oneOf.description: D26 agreement guard: identityAttestation selects the identity-attestation event literal.

recordKind.description: D26 agreement guard source: identity-attestation facts record kind.

event.description: D26 agreement guard target: identity-attestation event literal.

oneOf.description: Facts records outside the current D26 event-literal seed retain their existing open event vocabulary.

recordKind.description: Built-in facts record kinds that are outside the current D26 event-literal seed.

oneOf.description: Vendor-extension facts records retain their existing open event vocabulary.

recordKind.description: Vendor-extension facts record kind outside the current D26 event-literal seed.

OverrideRecord.description: Typed runtime payload appended to `FactsTierRecord` when `recordKind == "overrideRecorded"` (governance §7.3, workflow-governance.md:400-407). Mirrors the kernel `OverrideRecord` $def (`wos-workflow.schema.json#/$defs/OverrideRecord`); the API projection uses `caseInstanceRef` (URN per ADR 0082 D-4) rather than the kernel TypeID. Three required structured fields correspond to the three OverrideAuthority policy switches: `requireStructuredRationale → rationale`, `requireAuthorityVerification → authorityVerification`, `requireSupportingEvidence → supportingEvidence`. The audit query `recordKindFilter: ["overrideRecorded"]` returns `FactsTierRecord` envelopes carrying this payload in `overrideRecord`.

rationale.description: Structured human-readable rationale explaining the basis for the override. The audit trail always carries a one-line `summary`; agencies MAY cite regulatory authorities under `regulatoryCitations`.

summary.description: One-line human-readable explanation of why the override was issued. Surfaced in audit reports and adverse-decision notices.

regulatoryCitations.description: Optional citations to regulatory authorities, statutes, or policies that support the override. Free-form citation strings.

authorityVerification.description: Record of how the overriding actor's authority was verified (governance §7.3 item 2). Captures who verified, when, and against which delegation/policy reference. Processors that verify automatically (e.g., role lookup) MUST still populate this so the audit trail is complete.

actorRef.description: Actor URN that performed the override (D-9).

verifiedAt.description: RFC 3339 timestamp when the authority was verified.

delegationRef.description: Optional URN of the delegation grant that authorized this override (governance §11).

supportingEvidence.description: Evidence references supporting the override (governance §7.3 item 3). At least one entry MUST be present when the OverrideAuthority policy switch `requireSupportingEvidence` is true; conformant servers always populate it.

overriddenRecordRef.description: URN of the prior provenance record being overridden (e.g., the determination `stateTransition` whose outcome is being reversed).

NoticeSentRecord.description: Typed runtime payload appended to `FactsTierRecord` when `recordKind == "noticeSent"` (governance §3.2, workflow-governance.md:127). The byte-deterministic adverse-decision notice obligation requires that an applicant can retrieve the notice that was actually sent and verify hash equality against the Trellis-anchored copy. The four fields here are the load-bearing minimum: kind discriminator, content hash, recipient, and delivery timestamp. The full rendered notice body lives behind a `correspondenceRenderingRef` URN (claim-check pattern — D-3 subresource decomposition).

noticeKind.description: Closed-with-vendor-extension notice taxonomy mirroring the delivery-sidecar `NotificationTemplate.category` (correspondence.schema.json `RenderingCategory`). Reserved literals are normative due-process classifiers; vendor-extension form `x-<agency>-<category>` is permitted only when no reserved literal applies.

snapshotSha256.description: JCS-canonical SHA-256 hex digest of the notice's machine-readable artifact. Identical to the Facts-tier `caseFileSnapshot.sha256` for the determination that produced the notice (workflow-governance.md:127 byte-determinism requirement); applicants verify hash equality against the Trellis-anchored notice copy.

recipientActorRef.description: Actor URN of the notice recipient (typically the applicant, but may be a delegated representative or guardian per governance §11).

deliveryTimestamp.description: RFC 3339 timestamp when the notice was delivered (or when delivery was attempted for asynchronous channels). The transition-firing timestamp is captured separately on the parent `FactsTierRecord.timestamp`.

templateRef.description: Optional URN of the notification template used to render the notice (delivery-sidecar `NotificationTemplate`). Surfaced for audit traceability — the template + snapshot + transition-firing timestamp produce byte-identical notice bytes.

correspondenceRenderingRef.description: Optional URN of the rendered notice content (correspondence.schema.json `CorrespondenceRendering`). Claim-check pointer to the full prose; not inlined here to keep Facts-tier records small.

appealDeadline.description: Optional RFC 3339 appeal-window deadline derived deterministically from policy + transition-firing timestamp + business calendar. Present when the notice kind carries appeal rights (typically `adverseDecisionNotice`).

AuthorizationRejectedRecord.description: Typed public payload for `FactsTierRecord.authorizationRejectedRecord` when `recordKind == "authorizationRejected"` (ADR 0070 D-3). Captures who attempted which action against which resource and why policy denied it.

attemptedActorRef.description: Actor URN that attempted the rejected action.

attemptedAction.description: Action attempted by the actor, using the runtime's verb:resource convention.

targetResourceRef.description: Resource URN the rejected action targeted.

rejectionReason.description: Human-readable reason policy rejected the authorization attempt.

policyDecisionRef.description: Optional provenance or audit resource URN for the policy-engine decision that denied the attempt.

LegalHoldRecord.description: Typed public payload for legal-hold provenance: `legalHoldPlaced`, `legalHoldReleased`, and `legalHoldDestructionRejected`. Legal holds freeze destruction or adverse mutation until a lawful release occurs.

action.description: Legal-hold action represented by the parent record kind.

actorRef.description: Actor URN that placed, released, or enforced the legal hold.

scope.description: Case data, documents, or process surface covered by the legal hold.

effectiveAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the legal-hold action took effect.

reason.description: Human-readable basis for the legal hold or release.

destructionAttemptRef.description: Optional provenance record URN for the destruction attempt blocked by a legal hold.

CircuitBreakerEventRecord.description: Typed public payload for `circuitBreakerTripped` and `circuitBreakerReset` Facts records. Captures the governed agent breaker state transition and the threshold evidence behind it.

agentRef.description: Agent resource URN whose circuit breaker changed state.

breakerId.description: Identifier of the configured circuit breaker.

action.description: Circuit-breaker action represented by the parent record kind.

failureRate.description: Observed failure rate in the evaluation window, when available.

threshold.description: Configured failure-rate threshold that tripped or governed the reset.

cooldownUntil.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp after which the breaker may attempt half-open recovery.

ShadowModeDivergenceRecord.description: Typed public payload for `shadowModeDivergence`. Records a material divergence between a shadow-mode agent output and its configured baseline.

baseline.description: Configured baseline the shadow output was compared against, such as human or a production model identifier.

divergenceType.description: Closed classifier for the observed divergence.

severity.description: Operational severity assigned to the divergence.

magnitude.description: Normalized divergence magnitude, when the comparison method yields one.

examples.summary: Shadow agent recommended approval while the baseline reviewer denied.

DcrZoneViolationRecord.description: Typed public payload for `dcrZoneViolation`. Records a Dynamic Condition Response constraint-zone breach on a case instance.

zoneId.description: Identifier of the violated DCR constraint zone.

activityId.description: Workflow activity whose execution or omission violated the zone.

violationKind.description: DCR relation or state class that was violated.

examples.summary: Final decision attempted before independent appeal review completed.

DriftAlertRecord.description: Typed public payload for `driftAlert`. Records an alert threshold crossing from the workflow's drift monitoring policy.

agentRef.description: Agent resource URN whose drift monitor produced the alert.

driftStatus.description: Agent drift posture after the alert.

observedValue.description: Observed metric value that crossed the threshold.

threshold.description: Configured alert threshold for the metric.

policyRef.description: Optional drift policy reference that produced the alert.

ReportTimedOutRecord.description: Typed public payload for `reportTimedOut`. Records that a report run exceeded its server-enforced wall-clock ceiling and failed with WOS-1408.

reportRunRef.description: Report-run resource URN that timed out.

maxDurationSeconds.description: Resolved timeout ceiling applied to the run.

wosErrorCode.description: Canonical API error code for timeout exceeded.

QuorumReductionRecord.description: Typed public payload for explicit quorum-reduction provenance. Governance §S4.9 forbids silent quorum reduction; any reduction is a policy transition with a provenance-backed before/after record.

delegationRef.description: Delegation resource URN whose quorum requirement changed.

priorRequiredCount.description: Prior quorum count before the explicit reduction.

newRequiredCount.description: New quorum count after the explicit reduction.

authorityBasis.description: Policy, legal instrument, or supervisor authority authorizing the reduction.

actorRef.description: Actor URN that authorized the quorum reduction.

reason.description: Human-readable operational reason for the reduction.

examples.reason: one quorum member unavailable under declared emergency operations

ReasoningTierRecord.description: Reasoning-tier provenance record (governance S6.2). Authoritative interpretive layer recording how a decision was reached: which rules applied, which evidence was consulted, which criteria were checked. REQUIRED for transitions tagged `determination`.

id.description: Stable URN for this provenance record.

instanceId.description: Case instance URN this record belongs to.

recordKind.description: Underlying kernel record kind being interpreted (typically `stateTransition`).

timestamp.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the reasoning was recorded.

definitionVersion.description: Version of the governing Kernel Document at append time.

actorRef.description: Actor URN responsible for the recorded reasoning.

event.description: Triggering event for the underlying transition.

rulesApplied.description: Rules applied in this decision, with authority citations.

evidenceConsulted.description: Evidence references examined during this decision.

decisionRequirements.description: Declaration of required inputs and applicable rules (governance S6.3).

factsRecordRef.description: URN of the Facts-tier record this reasoning interprets.

assertionGates.description: Per-gate assertion results that fired during this reasoning step (governance §5.4 + §5.5; workflow-governance.md:293-309). Empty when the reasoning record did not run through a validation pipeline; populated when one or more gates evaluated. The pipeline's risk profile is determined by the weakest gate that passed (workflow-governance.md:309) — auditors read this array directly.

confidenceReport.description: Per-output confidence record (ai-integration.md §7.1). Present iff an agent produced the output this reasoning record interprets; absent for human-only reasoning steps.

CounterfactualTierRecord.description: Counterfactual-tier provenance record (governance S6.4). Authoritative due-process layer recording what would change the outcome (positive) and which protected characteristics did not affect the outcome (negative). REQUIRED for transitions tagged `adverse-decision` in `rights-impacting` workflows.

id.description: Stable URN for this provenance record.

instanceId.description: Case instance URN this record belongs to.

recordKind.description: Underlying kernel record kind being interpreted.

timestamp.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the counterfactual was recorded.

definitionVersion.description: Version of the governing Kernel Document at append time.

actorRef.description: Actor URN responsible for the recorded counterfactual analysis.

event.description: Triggering event for the underlying transition.

positiveCounterfactuals.description: Controllable factors that would change the outcome (governance S6.4).

negativeCounterfactuals.description: Irrelevant factors -- including protected characteristics -- that did NOT affect the outcome.

factsRecordRef.description: URN of the Facts-tier record this counterfactual analyzes.

NarrativeTierRecord.description: Narrative-tier provenance record (AI integration S13). Non-authoritative model-generated explanation. MUST carry `authoritative: false`. A conformant implementation MUST NOT treat narrative content as dispositive in adjudicative, audit, or due-process contexts (S13.2).

id.description: Stable URN for this provenance record.

instanceId.description: Case instance URN this record belongs to.

recordKind.description: Underlying kernel record kind being narrated.

timestamp.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the narrative was generated.

definitionVersion.description: Version of the governing Kernel Document at append time.

actorRef.description: Agent URN that generated the narrative.

event.description: Triggering event for the underlying transition.

authoritative.description: MUST be `false`. Explicitly marks this content as non-authoritative (S13.2).

modelIdentifier.description: Identifier of the model that generated this narrative.

modelVersion.description: Version of the model that generated this narrative.

factsRecordRef.description: URN of the Facts-tier record this narrative explains.

confidenceReport.description: Per-output confidence record for the model that generated this narrative (ai-integration.md §7.1 — "every agent output MUST be accompanied by a ConfidenceReport"; the narrative IS an agent output even though it is non-authoritative). Surfaced so consumers can gate narrative display on confidence (low-confidence narratives can be suppressed or annotated).

ProvenanceRecord.description: Tier-discriminated provenance record union (ADR 0082 D-5). The four tiers correspond to kernel Facts (S8), governance Reasoning (S6.2) and Counterfactual (S6.4), and AI integration Narrative (S13). The legacy single-envelope shape with four optional tier blocks (current `case-portal` `ProvenanceRecord`) is structurally inexpressible: every variant requires its own `tier` const literal and tier-specific fields are non-optional within their variant.

ProvenanceTimeRange.description: Inclusive UTC RFC 3339 time-range filter for `ProvenanceListOptions` (ADR 0069 wire format). Bounds the query to records whose `timestamp` falls within `[since, until]`. Mirrors the `AuditQueryRequest.timeRange` shape from `audit.schema.json` so cross-case audit and per-case provenance share the same filter contract.

since.description: Inclusive UTC RFC 3339 lower bound for the provenance window. Records with `timestamp >= since` are eligible.

until.description: Inclusive UTC RFC 3339 upper bound for the provenance window. Records with `timestamp <= until` are eligible.

ProvenanceListOptions.description: Query fields for `GET /api/v1/instances/{id}/provenance` (ADR 0082 D-7). Filters compose with AND semantics — a record matches when it satisfies every supplied filter independently. Mirrors the `AuditQueryRequest` filter shape from `audit.schema.json` so cross-case audit and per-case provenance use the same query patterns.

tier.description: Optional tier filter. When omitted, all tiers are returned.

recordKindFilter.description: Optional record-kind filter — restrict the page to one or more Facts-tier `recordKind` literals (for example `stateTransition`, `caseStateMutation`, `signatureAffirmation`). Cross-`$ref`s the closed-with-vendor-extension `FactsRecordKind` taxonomy declared in this schema. Empty array is forbidden; omit the field to span every record kind. Composes with AND semantics against `tier`, `timeRange`, and `actorRefFilter`.

timeRange.description: Optional inclusive UTC RFC 3339 time-range filter against the record `timestamp` field. Composes with AND semantics against the other filters; omit the field to span the full retention window.

actorRefFilter.description: Optional actor URN filter — restrict the page to records whose `actorRef` equals one of the supplied principals (ADR 0082 D-9). Cross-`$ref`s the canonical `ActorRef` from `_common.schema.json`. Empty array is forbidden. Composes with AND semantics against the other filters.

cursor.description: Opaque resume token returned by the previous page (ADR 0082 D-7). Cursor pagination still applies on top of the AND-composed filter set; cursors are opaque, single-use within the issuing deploy.

limit.description: Maximum number of records requested for this page.

items.description: Tier-discriminated provenance records in this page.

cursor.description: Opaque token for the next page. Omitted when hasMore is false.

hasMore.description: True when another page is available from the server.
